label: universe
definition: All existing matter and space considered as a whole

label: planet
definition: A celestial body orbiting a star

label: star
definition: A luminous sphere of plasma held together by gravity

label: galaxy
definition: A system of stars, stellar remnants, and interstellar matter

label: solar
definition: Relating to or determined by the sun

label: orbit
definition: The curved path of a celestial object around a star or planet

label: comet
definition: An icy celestial body that releases gas when passing near the sun

label: asteroid
definition: A small rocky body orbiting the sun

label: earth
definition: The third planet from the sun and the only known world with life

label: ocean
definition: A very large expanse of salt water covering most of Earth

label: mountain
definition: A large natural elevation of the earth's surface

label: river
definition: A large natural stream of water flowing in a channel

label: continent
definition: Any of the world's main continuous expanses of land

label: island
definition: A piece of land surrounded by water

label: history
definition: The study of past events and human affairs

label: civilization
definition: An advanced state of human society with complex culture and organization

label: empire
definition: An extensive group of states ruled over by a single authority

label: revolution
definition: A forcible overthrow of a government or social order

label: war
definition: A state of armed conflict between different nations or states

label: peace
definition: Freedom from disturbance; tranquility and harmony

label: science
definition: The systematic study of the structure and behavior of the natural world

label: physics
definition: The branch of science concerned with matter, energy, and their interactions

label: chemistry
definition: The branch of science dealing with substances and their properties

label: biology
definition: The study of living organisms and their vital processes

label: atom
definition: The smallest unit of matter that retains chemical properties

label: cell
definition: The smallest structural and functional unit of an organism

label: energy
definition: The capacity to do work or produce change

label: force
definition: An influence that causes an object to undergo a change

label: mythology
definition: A collection of myths belonging to a particular culture or tradition

label: god
definition: A supernatural being considered divine or sacred

label: hero
definition: A person admired for courage and noble qualities

label: legend
definition: A traditional story regarded as historical but unauthenticated

label: myth
definition: A traditional story explaining natural or social phenomena

label: dragon
definition: A mythical creature typically depicted as a large reptile with wings

label: literature
definition: Written works with artistic or intellectual value

label: novel
definition: A long fictional narrative in prose

label: poem
definition: A piece of writing with rhythm and often rhyme

label: story
definition: An account of imaginary or real events told for entertainment

label: author
definition: A person who writes books, articles, or other texts

label: book
definition: A written or printed work consisting of pages bound together

label: animal
definition: A living organism that feeds on organic matter

label: mammal
definition: A warm-blooded vertebrate animal with hair and mammary glands

label: bird
definition: A warm-blooded vertebrate with feathers and wings

label: fish
definition: A limbless cold-blooded vertebrate living in water

label: insect
definition: A small arthropod with six legs and a body divided into three parts

label: plant
definition: A living organism that typically grows in soil and has leaves

label: tree
definition: A woody perennial plant with a single main stem

label: flower
definition: The reproductive structure found in flowering plants

label: seed
definition: The unit of reproduction of a flowering plant

label: root
definition: The part of a plant that attaches it to the ground

label: Molecule
definition: A group of atoms bonded together, representing the smallest fundamental unit of a chemical compound that can participate in a chemical reaction.

metadata.etymology: From French 'molécule', from Latin 'molecula' (small mass), diminutive of 'moles' (mass)

label: Electron
definition: A subatomic particle with a negative electric charge that orbits the nucleus of an atom.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'elektron' meaning 'amber' (which produces static electricity when rubbed)

label: Proton
definition: A stable subatomic particle with a positive electric charge found in the nucleus of every atom.

label: Neutron
definition: A subatomic particle with no electric charge found in the nucleus of atoms, having approximately the same mass as a proton.

label: DNA
definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid, a molecule carrying genetic instructions for the development, functioning, growth, and reproduction of all known organisms.

metadata.etymology: Abbreviation of 'deoxyribonucleic acid', from chemical components

label: Evolution
definition: The process by which different kinds of living organisms develop and diversify from earlier forms through genetic changes over successive generations.

label: Photosynthesis
definition: The process by which green plants and some organisms use sunlight to synthesize nutrients from carbon dioxide and water, producing oxygen as a byproduct.

label: Gravity
definition: The force that attracts a body toward the center of the earth or toward any other physical body having mass.

label: Enzyme
definition: A biological catalyst, typically a protein, that speeds up chemical reactions in living organisms without being consumed in the process.

label: Catalyst
definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without itself undergoing permanent chemical change.

label: Protein
definition: Large biomolecules consisting of chains of amino acids, essential for the structure, function, and regulation of body tissues and organs.

metadata.etymology: From French 'protéine', from Greek 'proteios' meaning 'primary', from 'protos' (first)

label: Carbon
definition: A nonmetallic chemical element with atomic number 6, forming the basis of all organic compounds and essential to life.

label: Oxygen
definition: A colorless, odorless gas with atomic number 8, essential for respiration in most living organisms and supporting combustion.

label: Hydrogen
definition: The lightest and most abundant chemical element with atomic number 1, forming water when combined with oxygen.

label: Nucleus
definition: The central and most important part of an object or group, forming the basis for activity or growth; in biology, the membrane-bound organelle containing genetic material; in physics, the dense central core of an atom.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'nucleus' meaning 'kernel, inner part', diminutive of 'nux' (nut)

label: Organism
definition: An individual living thing, such as an animal, plant, fungus, or bacterium, capable of carrying on life processes.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'organon' meaning 'instrument, tool' + '-ismos' (suffix forming nouns)

label: Species
definition: A group of living organisms consisting of similar individuals capable of exchanging genes or interbreeding, the basic unit of biological classification.

label: Ecosystem
definition: A biological community of interacting organisms and their physical environment, functioning as a unit.

label: Reaction
definition: A process in which one or more substances are changed into others through the breaking and forming of chemical bonds.

label: Velocity
definition: The speed of something in a given direction, a vector quantity measuring the rate of change of position.

label: Mass
definition: A measure of the amount of matter in an object, determining its resistance to acceleration and gravitational attraction to other bodies.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'massa' meaning 'lump, dough', from Greek 'maza' (barley cake)

label: Temperature
definition: A physical quantity expressing hot and cold, measuring the average kinetic energy of particles in a substance.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'temperatura' meaning 'a tempering, moderation', from 'temperare' (to mix, regulate)

label: Radiation
definition: The emission or transmission of energy in the form of waves or particles through space or a medium.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'radiare' meaning 'to emit rays', from 'radius' (ray, beam)

label: Magnetism
definition: A physical phenomenon produced by moving electric charges, resulting in attractive and repulsive forces between objects.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Magnesia' (region in Thessaly where magnetic stones were found)

label: Electricity
definition: A form of energy resulting from the movement of charged particles, such as electrons, manifesting as lightning, static electricity, or electric current.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'electricus' meaning 'of amber', from Greek 'elektron' (amber)

label: Wavelength
definition: The distance between successive crests of a wave, especially in electromagnetic radiation or sound waves.

label: Frequency
definition: The rate at which something occurs or is repeated over a particular period, especially the number of cycles per second of a wave.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'frequentia' meaning 'crowd, multitude', from 'frequens' (crowded, repeated)

label: Compound
definition: A substance formed when two or more chemical elements are chemically bonded together in fixed proportions.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'componere' meaning 'to put together', from 'com-' (together) + 'ponere' (to place)

label: Solution
definition: A homogeneous mixture composed of two or more substances, where a solute is dissolved in a solvent.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'solutio' meaning 'a loosening, dissolving', from 'solvere' (to loosen, dissolve)

label: Acid
definition: A substance with a pH less than 7 that can donate hydrogen ions, typically having a sour taste and turning litmus paper red.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'acidus' meaning 'sour', from 'acere' (to be sour)

label: Base
definition: A substance with a pH greater than 7 that can accept hydrogen ions, typically feeling slippery and turning litmus paper blue.

label: Ion
definition: An atom or molecule with a net electric charge due to the loss or gain of one or more electrons.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'ion', present participle of 'ienai' meaning 'to go'

label: Bond
definition: A lasting attraction between atoms that enables the formation of chemical compounds, typically involving sharing or transfer of electrons.

metadata.etymology: From Middle English 'band', from Old Norse 'band' (that which binds)

label: Membrane
definition: A thin, flexible sheet of tissue or material that separates or connects structures, especially the selectively permeable barrier surrounding cells.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'membrana' meaning 'skin covering a limb', from 'membrum' (limb)

label: Chromosome
definition: A thread-like structure of DNA and proteins found in the nucleus of cells, carrying genetic information in the form of genes.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'chroma' (color) + 'soma' (body), named for staining properties

label: Gene
definition: A unit of heredity made up of DNA that occupies a specific location on a chromosome and determines a particular characteristic in an organism.

metadata.etymology: From German 'Gen', shortened from 'Pangen', from Greek 'genos' (race, offspring)

label: Respiration
definition: The biochemical process in which cells break down glucose and oxygen to release energy, producing carbon dioxide and water as byproducts.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'respirare' meaning 'to breathe', from 're-' (again) + 'spirare' (to breathe)

label: Metabolism
definition: The chemical processes that occur within living organisms to maintain life, including converting food to energy and building cellular materials.

label: Mutation
definition: A change in the DNA sequence of a gene that can occur spontaneously or be induced, potentially altering the organism's characteristics.

label: Darwin
definition: Charles Darwin (1809-1882), English naturalist who established the theory of evolution through natural selection in his work 'On the Origin of Species'.

metadata.etymology: English surname, from Old English 'Deorwine' meaning 'dear friend'

label: Newton
definition: Isaac Newton (1643-1727), English mathematician and physicist who formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation, revolutionizing physics.

metadata.etymology: English surname meaning 'new town', from Old English 'niwe' + 'tun'

label: Einstein
definition: Albert Einstein (1879-1955), German-born theoretical physicist who developed the theory of relativity, fundamentally changing our understanding of space, time, and energy.

label: Quantum
definition: The minimum amount of any physical entity involved in an interaction, representing the discrete nature of energy and matter at atomic scales.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'quantum' meaning 'how much', neuter of 'quantus' (how great)

label: body
definition: The physical structure of a person or animal, including bones, flesh, and organs

metadata.etymology: Old English 'bodig', meaning trunk or chest

label: mind
definition: The element of a person that enables them to be aware of the world and their experiences, to think, and to feel

label: emotion
definition: A strong feeling deriving from one's circumstances, mood, or relationships with others

metadata.etymology: Latin 'emovere', meaning to move out, agitate

label: health
definition: The state of being free from illness or injury; overall physical and mental well-being

metadata.etymology: Old English 'hælth', meaning wholeness, being whole

label: sense
definition: A faculty by which the body perceives an external stimulus; one of the five traditional senses

label: heart
definition: A hollow muscular organ that pumps blood through the circulatory system by rhythmic contraction and dilation

label: brain
definition: The organ of the central nervous system responsible for thought, memory, emotion, and sensory processing

label: lung
definition: Either of the two respiratory organs that oxygenate blood and remove carbon dioxide

label: liver
definition: A large organ that detoxifies chemicals, metabolizes drugs, and produces biochemicals for digestion

label: kidney
definition: Either of a pair of organs that filter waste products from the blood and produce urine

metadata.etymology: Middle English 'kidnei', compound of unknown origin

label: stomach
definition: The internal organ where food is digested before passing to the intestines

label: eye
definition: The organ of sight in humans and animals, detecting light and converting it to neural signals

label: ear
definition: The organ of hearing and balance in humans and animals

label: nose
definition: The organ of smell and the entrance to the respiratory tract

label: tongue
definition: The muscular organ in the mouth used for tasting, swallowing, and speech

label: skin
definition: The outer covering of the body, the largest organ, providing protection and sensation

label: vision
definition: The faculty or state of being able to see; sight

label: hearing
definition: The faculty of perceiving sounds

label: touch
definition: The faculty of perception through physical contact with the skin

label: taste
definition: The sensation of flavor perceived in the mouth and throat on contact with a substance

metadata.etymology: Old French 'taster', meaning to handle, touch, taste

label: smell
definition: The faculty of perceiving odors by means of the olfactory nerves

label: pain
definition: Physical suffering or discomfort caused by illness or injury

label: pleasure
definition: A feeling of happy satisfaction and enjoyment

label: joy
definition: A feeling of great pleasure and happiness

label: sadness
definition: The condition or quality of being sad; sorrow

label: anger
definition: A strong feeling of annoyance, displeasure, or hostility

label: fear
definition: An unpleasant emotion caused by the belief that something is dangerous or threatening

label: anxiety
definition: A feeling of worry, nervousness, or unease about something with an uncertain outcome

label: love
definition: An intense feeling of deep affection or strong attachment to someone or something

label: stress
definition: A state of mental or emotional strain resulting from demanding circumstances

label: depression
definition: A mental health disorder characterized by persistently depressed mood or loss of interest

label: memory
definition: The faculty by which the mind stores and remembers information

label: consciousness
definition: The state of being awake and aware of one's surroundings and thoughts

label: breath
definition: The air taken into or expelled from the lungs during respiration

label: pulse
definition: The rhythmic throbbing of arteries as blood is propelled through them

label: muscle
definition: A tissue composed of cells capable of contracting to produce movement

metadata.etymology: Latin 'musculus', meaning little mouse (from muscle movement)

label: bone
definition: Any of the hard pieces of tissue making up the skeleton in vertebrates

label: blood
definition: The red liquid that circulates through the body carrying oxygen and nutrients

label: nerve
definition: A fiber or bundle of fibers that transmits impulses between the brain and body

label: immune
definition: Protected against a particular disease or pathogen by antibodies or white blood cells

label: society
definition: An organized group of people living together in a community with shared laws, traditions, and values

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'societas' meaning fellowship, from 'socius' meaning companion

label: economy
definition: The system of production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services in a society

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'oikonomia' meaning household management, from 'oikos' (house) + 'nomos' (law)

label: education
definition: The process of facilitating learning, acquiring knowledge, skills, values, and habits

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'educatio' meaning a bringing up, from 'educare' to lead out, train

label: government
definition: The system or group of people governing an organized community, typically a state

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'governement' from Latin 'gubernare' to steer, rule

label: law
definition: A system of rules created and enforced through social or governmental institutions to regulate behavior

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'lagu' from Old Norse 'lag' meaning something laid down or fixed

label: democracy
definition: A system of government in which power is vested in the people, who rule either directly or through elected representatives

label: capitalism
definition: An economic system based on private ownership of the means of production and their operation for profit

metadata.etymology: From 'capital' from Latin 'capitalis' meaning of the head, principal, from 'caput' (head)

label: socialism
definition: A political and economic theory advocating collective or governmental ownership and administration of production and distribution

metadata.etymology: From French 'socialisme' from 'social' relating to society

label: citizen
definition: A legally recognized member of a state or nation with associated rights and obligations

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'citezein' from 'cité' (city), ultimately from Latin 'civitas'

label: constitution
definition: The fundamental principles or established precedents according to which a state is governed

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'constitutio' meaning establishment, from 'constituere' to set up

label: parliament
definition: A legislative body of government, typically consisting of elected representatives

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'parlement' meaning speaking, discussion, from 'parler' to speak

label: congress
definition: A formal meeting or assembly of representatives for discussion and decision-making, especially a national legislative body

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'congressus' meaning a meeting, from 'con-' (together) + 'gradi' (to walk)

label: election
definition: A formal process by which people choose a person or group to hold public office

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'electio' meaning choice, selection, from 'eligere' to pick out

label: policy
definition: A course or principle of action adopted or proposed by a government, party, business, or individual

metadata.etymology: From Middle French 'police' meaning government, from Latin 'politia' from Greek 'politeia'

label: taxation
definition: The imposition of compulsory levies on individuals or entities by governments to fund public expenditure

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'taxare' meaning to estimate, assess, from 'tangere' to touch

label: budget
definition: An estimate of income and expenditure for a set period of time, especially for government operations

metadata.etymology: From Middle French 'bougette' meaning small bag, diminutive of 'bouge' leather bag

label: inflation
definition: A sustained increase in the general price level of goods and services, reducing purchasing power

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'inflatio' meaning a puffing up, from 'inflare' to blow into

label: unemployment
definition: The state of being without paid work but available and seeking employment

metadata.etymology: From 'un-' (not) + 'employment' from French 'emploi' meaning use, work

label: GDP
definition: Gross Domestic Product - the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country during a specific period

metadata.etymology: Acronym from Gross Domestic Product, 'gross' from Latin 'grossus' meaning thick, large

label: trade
definition: The action of buying and selling goods and services between countries or entities

metadata.etymology: From Middle Low German 'trade' meaning track, course

label: market
definition: A system or place where buyers and sellers interact to exchange goods, services, or information

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'mercatus' meaning trading, marketplace, from 'mercari' to trade

label: currency
definition: A system of money in general use in a particular country or economic context

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'currens' meaning running, flowing, present participle of 'currere' to run

label: regulation
definition: A rule or directive made and maintained by an authority to control or govern conduct

metadata.etymology: From Late Latin 'regulatio' meaning a regulating, from 'regulare' to control by rule

label: justice
definition: The quality of being fair and reasonable, or the administration of law according to prescribed principles

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'justitia' meaning righteousness, equity, from 'justus' meaning just, right

label: court
definition: A tribunal presided over by a judge or judges with authority to hear and decide legal cases

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'court' from Latin 'cohors' meaning enclosed yard, company of soldiers

label: judge
definition: A public official appointed to decide cases in a court of law

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'jugier' from Latin 'judicare' to judge, from 'judex' meaning judge

label: rights
definition: Legal, social, or ethical principles of freedom or entitlement that belong to individuals or groups

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'riht' meaning just, good, proper, from Proto-Germanic 'rekhtaz'

label: freedom
definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants without hindrance or restraint

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'freodom' meaning power of self-determination, from 'freo' meaning free

label: literacy
definition: The ability to read and write, or competence in a particular area of knowledge

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'litteratus' meaning educated, learned, from 'littera' meaning letter

label: curriculum
definition: The subjects comprising a course of study in a school or college

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'curriculum' meaning a running, course, career, from 'currere' to run

label: university
definition: An institution of higher education and research that grants academic degrees

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'universitas' meaning the whole, aggregate, from 'universus' meaning whole

label: scholarship
definition: A grant or payment made to support a student's education, or academic achievement and learning

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'scolere' meaning student + 'ship' indicating state or condition

label: diploma
definition: A certificate awarded by an educational institution to show that someone has successfully completed a course of study

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'diploma' from Greek 'diploma' meaning folded paper, letter of recommendation

label: tuition
definition: The fee charged for teaching or instruction, especially at a formal educational institution

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'tuitio' meaning a looking after, protection, from 'tueri' to watch, guard

label: pedagogy
definition: The method and practice of teaching, especially as an academic subject or theoretical concept

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'paidagogos' meaning teacher of children, from 'pais' (child) + 'agogos' (leader)

label: welfare
definition: Statutory procedure or social effort designed to promote the basic well-being of people in need

metadata.etymology: From Middle English 'wel faren' meaning to fare well, from 'wel' (well) + 'faren' (to go)

label: equality
definition: The state of being equal in status, rights, and opportunities

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'aequalitas' from 'aequalis' meaning equal, from 'aequus' level, even

label: poverty
definition: The state of being extremely poor, lacking sufficient money or resources for basic living conditions

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'poverte' from Latin 'paupertas' meaning poverty, from 'pauper' poor

label: prosperity
definition: The state of being prosperous, successful, or thriving, especially in financial terms

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'prosperitas' from 'prosperus' meaning doing well, favorable

label: reform
definition: The action or process of reforming an institution or practice to improve it

metadata.etymology: From Latin 're-' (again) + 'formare' to form, from 'forma' meaning form, shape

label: time
definition: The indefinite continued progress of existence and events in the past, present, and future regarded as a whole.

label: season
definition: Each of the four divisions of the year (spring, summer, autumn, and winter) marked by particular weather patterns and daylight hours.

metadata.etymology: From Old French 'seison', from Latin 'satio' meaning sowing or planting

label: cycle
definition: A series of events that are regularly repeated in the same order, returning to the starting point.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'kyklos' meaning circle or wheel

label: period
definition: A length or portion of time with a particular characteristic or defined by specific events.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'periodos' meaning recurring cycle, circuit

label: century
definition: A period of one hundred years, used as a unit for measuring historical time.

label: decade
definition: A period of ten years, especially one beginning with a year ending in 0.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'dekas' meaning group of ten

label: millennium
definition: A period of one thousand years, especially when calculated from the birth of Christ.

label: era
definition: A long and distinct period of history with a particular feature or characteristic.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'aera' meaning counters used for calculation, fixed date

label: epoch
definition: A particular period of time in history or a person's life marked by notable events or characteristics.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'epokhe' meaning fixed point in time, cessation

label: chronology
definition: The science of arranging events in their order of occurrence in time and the study of time measurement.

label: eternal
definition: Lasting or existing forever, without end or beginning; timeless.

label: temporal
definition: Relating to worldly as opposed to spiritual affairs; relating to time.

label: antiquity
definition: The ancient past, especially the period before the Middle Ages; great age.

label: medieval
definition: Relating to the Middle Ages, the period of European history from about 500 to 1500 AD.

label: contemporary
definition: Living or occurring at the same time; belonging to or occurring in the present.

label: solstice
definition: Either of the two times in the year when the sun reaches its highest or lowest point in the sky at noon, marking the longest and shortest days.

label: equinox
definition: The time or date twice each year when day and night are of equal length, occurring in spring and autumn.

label: perennial
definition: Lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; enduring or continually recurring.

label: chronological
definition: Following the order in which events occurred; arranged in the order of time.

label: anachronism
definition: A thing belonging or appropriate to a period other than that in which it exists, especially a thing that is conspicuously old-fashioned.

label: prehistoric
definition: Relating to or denoting the period before written records, before history was documented.

label: renaissance
definition: The revival of art and literature under the influence of classical models in the 14th-16th centuries; a revival or renewed interest.

metadata.etymology: From French, literally 'rebirth', from Latin 're-' (again) + 'nasci' (be born)

label: duration
definition: The time during which something continues or exists; the length of time something lasts.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'durare' meaning to last, harden

label: interim
definition: The intervening time between two periods; a temporary or provisional arrangement.

label: perpetual
definition: Never ending or changing; occurring repeatedly without interruption; continuous.

label: transient
definition: Lasting only for a short time; impermanent; passing quickly.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'transire' meaning to go across, pass

label: chronometer
definition: An instrument for measuring time, especially one designed to be accurate in all conditions.

label: synchronize
definition: To cause to occur or operate at the same time or rate; to coordinate in time.

label: vicissitude
definition: A change of circumstances or fortune, typically one that is unwelcome or unpleasant; alternation between opposite states.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'vicissitudo', from 'vicis' meaning change, turn

label: philosophy
definition: The study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, values, reason, mind, and language through critical and systematic approaches.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'philosophia' meaning 'love of wisdom', from 'philos' (loving) + 'sophia' (wisdom)

label: logic
definition: The systematic study of valid reasoning and inference, including the principles that distinguish correct from incorrect arguments.

label: ethics
definition: The branch of philosophy that studies moral principles, values, and codes of conduct that govern individual and collective behavior.

label: truth
definition: The quality or state of being in accordance with fact or reality; a verified or indisputable fact, proposition, or principle.

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'triewth' meaning 'faithfulness, veracity', related to 'true'

label: wisdom
definition: The ability to apply knowledge, experience, and good judgment to make sound decisions and understand deep truths about life and nature.

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'wisdom', from 'wis' (wise) + '-dom' (suffix denoting state or condition)

label: metaphysics
definition: The branch of philosophy that examines the fundamental nature of reality, including the relationship between mind and matter, substance and attribute, and possibility and actuality.

label: epistemology
definition: The philosophical study of knowledge, including its nature, sources, limitations, and validity.

label: morality
definition: Principles concerning the distinction between right and wrong or good and bad behavior; a system of values and principles of conduct.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'moralitas' meaning 'manner, character', from 'mos' (custom, manner)

label: virtue
definition: A morally excellent quality or trait; behavior showing high moral standards such as courage, compassion, honesty, and justice.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'virtus' meaning 'moral strength, excellence', from 'vir' (man)

label: reason
definition: The capacity for logical, rational thought and inference; the mental faculty that enables humans to think, understand, and form judgments logically.

label: dialectic
definition: A method of philosophical argument that involves contradiction and resolution, typically used to establish truth through reasoned discussion between opposing viewpoints.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'dialektike' meaning 'art of debate', from 'dialegesthai' (to converse)

label: syllogism
definition: A form of deductive reasoning consisting of a major premise, a minor premise, and a conclusion that logically follows from the two premises.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'syllogismos' meaning 'inference, deduction', from 'syllogizesthai' (to reckon together)

label: premise
definition: A proposition or statement that forms the basis for an argument or theory; an assumption upon which reasoning is based.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'praemissa' meaning 'things mentioned before', from 'praemittere' (to send before)

label: inference
definition: The process of deriving logical conclusions from premises known or assumed to be true; reasoning from evidence to reach a conclusion.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'inferre' meaning 'to bring in, carry in', from 'in-' + 'ferre' (to carry)

label: fallacy
definition: A mistaken belief or flawed argument based on unsound reasoning; an error in logic that renders an argument invalid.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'fallacia' meaning 'deception, trick', from 'fallere' (to deceive)

label: deduction
definition: A form of logical reasoning that moves from general principles to specific conclusions; if the premises are true, the conclusion must be true.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'deductio' meaning 'a leading down', from 'deducere' (to lead down)

label: induction
definition: A form of reasoning that moves from specific observations to broader generalizations; reasoning from particular instances to universal principles.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'inductio' meaning 'a leading in', from 'inducere' (to lead in)

label: axiom
definition: A self-evident truth or fundamental principle that is accepted without proof and serves as a starting point for further reasoning.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'axioma' meaning 'that which is thought worthy', from 'axios' (worthy)

label: paradox
definition: A statement or proposition that seems self-contradictory or absurd but may express a possible truth; a situation with contradictory yet interrelated elements.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'paradoxon' meaning 'contrary to expectation', from 'para' (beyond) + 'doxa' (opinion)

label: utilitarianism
definition: An ethical theory that determines right from wrong by focusing on outcomes; actions are morally right if they maximize overall happiness or utility.

metadata.etymology: From 'utility' + '-arian' + '-ism', coined in 19th century to describe consequentialist ethics

label: categorical imperative
definition: Kant's principle that one should act only according to maxims that could become universal laws; an unconditional moral obligation binding in all circumstances.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'categoricus' (absolute) + 'imperativus' (commanding), formulated by Immanuel Kant

label: empiricism
definition: The philosophical theory that all knowledge is derived from sensory experience; the view that evidence from the senses is the primary source of knowledge.

label: rationalism
definition: The philosophical view that reason and innate ideas, rather than sensory experience, are the primary sources of knowledge and justification.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'rationalis' meaning 'of or belonging to reason', from 'ratio' (reason)

label: skepticism
definition: The philosophical position that questions the possibility of certainty in knowledge; the practice of doubting claims and requiring evidence before acceptance.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'skeptikos' meaning 'inquiring, reflective', from 'skeptesthai' (to look, consider)

label: ontology
definition: The philosophical study of being, existence, and reality; the branch of metaphysics concerned with the nature and categories of what exists.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'on' (being) + 'logos' (study), meaning 'the study of being'

label: determinism
definition: The philosophical view that all events, including human actions, are ultimately determined by causes external to the will; the doctrine that every event has a cause.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'determinare' meaning 'to set bounds to, limit', from 'de-' + 'terminus' (boundary)

label: free will
definition: The capacity of agents to choose between different possible courses of action unimpeded; the power to make choices unconstrained by external forces or fate.

metadata.etymology: From Old English 'freo' (free) + 'will' (desire, intention), expressing autonomous choice

label: consciousness
definition: The state of being aware of and responsive to one's surroundings; the quality of subjective experience and awareness of internal and external existence.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'conscius' meaning 'knowing with others, aware', from 'con-' (with) + 'scire' (to know)

label: justice
definition: The quality of being fair and reasonable; the principle of moral rightness and equitable treatment based on ethics, rationality, law, and fairness.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'justitia' meaning 'righteousness, equity', from 'justus' (just, fair)

label: existentialism
definition: A philosophical movement emphasizing individual existence, freedom, and choice; the view that individuals create their own meaning and essence through free will and actions.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'existere' meaning 'to come into being', emphasizing existence preceding essence

label: nihilism
definition: The philosophical belief that life is without objective meaning, purpose, or intrinsic value; the rejection of all religious and moral principles.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'nihil' meaning 'nothing', expressing the negation of meaning and value

label: altruism
definition: The principle or practice of unselfish concern for or devotion to the welfare of others; behavior motivated by concern for others rather than self-interest.

metadata.etymology: From French 'altruisme', from Italian 'altrui' meaning 'of others', from Latin 'alter' (other)

label: autonomy
definition: The capacity to make informed, uncoerced decisions; self-governance and the right to self-determination without external control or interference.

label: doctrine
definition: A set of beliefs or principles taught and advocated by a philosophical, religious, or political group; a body of teachings on a particular subject.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'doctrina' meaning 'teaching, instruction', from 'docere' (to teach)

label: ideology
definition: A system of ideas and ideals forming the basis of economic or political theory and policy; a comprehensive vision or worldview guiding individual or collective action.

label: sophistry
definition: The use of clever but false arguments, especially with the intention to deceive; reasoning that seems plausible but is actually fallacious or misleading.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'sophistes' meaning 'wise man, expert', later acquiring negative connotation of deceitful reasoning

label: stoicism
definition: An ancient Greek philosophy teaching that virtue, the highest good, is based on knowledge; advocates accepting events calmly and cultivating self-control and fortitude.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Stoa' (porch), where the philosopher Zeno taught, meaning 'of the porch'

label: hedonism
definition: The ethical theory that pleasure or happiness is the highest good and proper aim of human life; the pursuit of pleasure as a way of life.

label: pragmatism
definition: A philosophical tradition that views truth as what works in practice; the doctrine that the meaning and truth of ideas lie in their practical consequences and applications.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'pragma' meaning 'deed, act', emphasizing practical action over theory

label: transcendence
definition: The state of existing beyond or above the physical or material realm; surpassing usual limits or going beyond ordinary experience or understanding.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'transcendere' meaning 'to climb over, surpass', from 'trans-' (across) + 'scandere' (to climb)

label: computer
definition: An electronic device that processes data according to programmed instructions, capable of storing, retrieving, and manipulating information.

label: internet
definition: A global network of interconnected computers that communicate using standardized protocols, enabling information sharing and digital services worldwide.

label: software
definition: A collection of programs, data, and instructions that tell a computer how to perform specific tasks, as opposed to physical hardware.

metadata.etymology: Coined in 1958 by statistician John Tukey as contrast to 'hardware'

label: data
definition: Facts, statistics, or information collected together for reference, analysis, or processing by computer systems.

label: algorithm
definition: A step-by-step procedure or formula for solving a problem or completing a task, especially by a computer.

metadata.etymology: From name of Persian mathematician Al-Khwarizmi (9th century)

label: artificial intelligence
definition: The simulation of human intelligence processes by machines, especially computer systems, including learning, reasoning, and self-correction.

metadata.etymology: Term coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at Dartmouth Conference

label: machine learning
definition: A subset of AI that enables systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed.

metadata.etymology: Term popularized by Arthur Samuel in 1959

label: neural network
definition: A computing system inspired by biological neural networks, consisting of interconnected nodes that process information.

metadata.etymology: Named after biological neurons in animal brains

label: database
definition: An organized collection of structured data stored electronically, typically accessed and managed through a database management system.

label: programming
definition: The process of writing instructions for computers to execute, using specific programming languages and syntax.

label: website
definition: A collection of related web pages located under a single domain name, accessible via the internet.

label: server
definition: A computer or system that provides resources, data, services, or programs to other computers (clients) over a network.

label: cloud computing
definition: The delivery of computing services over the internet, including storage, processing power, and applications.

metadata.etymology: Cloud metaphor from network diagrams in 1990s

label: encryption
definition: The process of converting information into a coded format to prevent unauthorized access.

label: cybersecurity
definition: The practice of protecting systems, networks, and programs from digital attacks and unauthorized access.

label: protocol
definition: A set of rules governing the exchange or transmission of data between devices in a network.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'protokollon' meaning 'first sheet glued to manuscript'

label: bandwidth
definition: The maximum rate of data transfer across a given path, typically measured in bits per second.

label: debugging
definition: The process of finding and fixing errors or bugs in computer programs.

metadata.etymology: Attributed to Grace Hopper removing a moth from computer in 1947

label: API
definition: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications and enabling communication between different programs.

label: binary
definition: A number system using only two digits (0 and 1), forming the foundation of all computer operations.

label: CPU
definition: Central Processing Unit - the primary component of a computer that performs instructions and processes data.

metadata.etymology: Term emerged in 1960s with computer architecture

label: memory
definition: Computer hardware that stores data and instructions for quick access by the processor.

label: operating system
definition: System software that manages computer hardware and provides services for computer programs.

label: compiler
definition: A program that translates source code written in a high-level programming language into machine code.

label: framework
definition: A platform providing a foundation and structure for developing software applications with pre-built components.

label: open source
definition: Software with source code that anyone can inspect, modify, and enhance, typically developed collaboratively.

metadata.etymology: Term coined in 1998 by Christine Peterson

label: repository
definition: A central location where code and related files are stored and managed, often using version control systems.

label: blockchain
definition: A distributed ledger technology that records transactions across multiple computers in a tamper-resistant manner.

metadata.etymology: Compound describing chain of blocks containing data

label: virtualization
definition: Technology that creates simulated computing environments, allowing multiple virtual systems to run on a single physical machine.

label: latency
definition: The time delay between a request and response in a computer system or network.

label: pixel
definition: The smallest controllable element of a digital image or display, short for 'picture element'.

label: router
definition: A networking device that forwards data packets between computer networks.

label: syntax
definition: The set of rules that defines the combinations of symbols in a programming language.

label: cache
definition: A hardware or software component that stores data temporarily to speed up future requests for that data.

label: authentication
definition: The process of verifying the identity of a user or system before granting access.

label: scalability
definition: The capability of a system to handle growing amounts of work or accommodate expansion.

label: backup
definition: A copy of data stored separately to protect against loss or corruption of the original.

label: firewall
definition: A network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on security rules.

metadata.etymology: Metaphor from physical fire-prevention walls in buildings

label: kernel
definition: The core component of an operating system that manages system resources and hardware-software communication.

label: malware
definition: Malicious software designed to damage, disrupt, or gain unauthorized access to computer systems.

label: throttling
definition: The intentional slowing or limiting of data transfer rate in a network or API to prevent overload.

label: interface
definition: A point of interaction between a user and a computer system, or between different software components.

label: render
definition: The process of generating an image or display from a model or data structure.

label: query
definition: A request for information or data from a database or system.

label: recursion
definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve a problem by breaking it into smaller instances.

label: refactoring
definition: The process of restructuring existing code without changing its external behavior to improve readability and maintainability.

label: throughput
definition: The amount of data or number of operations processed by a system in a given time period.

label: token
definition: A piece of data used for authentication or as a basic unit in text processing and programming languages.

label: deployment
definition: The process of making software available for use in a production environment.

metadata.etymology: From French 'deployer' meaning 'to unfold, spread out'

label: pipeline
definition: A series of automated processes that move data or code through stages of processing, testing, or deployment.

metadata.etymology: Metaphor from physical pipelines for fluid transport

label: art
definition: The expression or application of human creative skill and imagination, typically in visual form such as painting or sculpture, producing works to be appreciated primarily for their beauty or emotional power.

label: music
definition: The art of arranging sounds in time through the elements of melody, harmony, rhythm, and timbre to express ideas and emotions.

label: culture
definition: The arts, customs, and habits that characterize a particular society or nation; the collective manifestation of human intellectual achievement.

label: painting
definition: The practice of applying pigment suspended in a medium to a surface such as canvas, paper, or wall, creating visual art through color and form.

label: sculpture
definition: The art of making three-dimensional representative or abstract forms by carving, modeling, or casting in various materials such as stone, metal, or wood.

label: symphony
definition: An elaborate musical composition for full orchestra, typically in four movements, at least one of which is in sonata form.

label: poetry
definition: Literary work in which special intensity is given to the expression of feelings and ideas by the use of distinctive style, rhythm, and metaphor.

label: Renaissance
definition: The revival of art and literature under the influence of classical models in the 14th-16th centuries, marking the transition from medieval to modern times in Europe.

label: Shakespeare
definition: William Shakespeare (1564-1616), English playwright and poet, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language and the world's greatest dramatist.

label: Mozart
definition: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791), prolific and influential Austrian composer of the Classical period, known for his mastery of all musical genres.

label: museum
definition: A building in which objects of historical, scientific, artistic, or cultural interest are stored and exhibited to the public.

label: canvas
definition: A strong, coarse unbleached cloth made from hemp, flax, or cotton, used especially for painting on or as a surface for oil paintings.

label: melody
definition: A sequence of single notes that is musically satisfying; the principal part in harmonized music; a tune.

label: rhythm
definition: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of movement or sound in music; the systematic arrangement of musical sounds according to duration and periodic stress.

label: masterpiece
definition: A work of outstanding artistry, skill, or workmanship; an artist's or craftsman's best piece of work.

label: gallery
definition: A room or building for the display or sale of works of art; a collection of pictures or other artworks.

label: portrait
definition: A painting, drawing, photograph, or engraving of a person, especially one depicting only the face or head and shoulders.

label: composer
definition: A person who writes music, especially as a professional occupation; one who creates original musical compositions.

label: orchestra
definition: A large group of musicians who play together on various instruments, typically including strings, woodwinds, brass, and percussion sections.

label: drama
definition: A play for theater, radio, or television; the art or activity of writing and performing plays.

label: manuscript
definition: A book, document, or piece of music written by hand rather than typed or printed; an author's original or draft version of a text.

label: aesthetic
definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty; relating to the philosophy of aesthetics and the study of art and beauty.

label: ballad
definition: A poem or song narrating a story in short stanzas; a slow sentimental or romantic song.

label: Baroque
definition: A highly ornate and often extravagant style of architecture, art, and music that flourished in Europe from the early 17th to mid-18th century.

label: classical
definition: Relating to ancient Greek or Roman culture; representing an exemplary standard within a traditional and long-established form or style.

label: metaphor
definition: A figure of speech in which a word or phrase is applied to an object or action to which it is not literally applicable, creating an implicit comparison.

label: sonnet
definition: A poem of fourteen lines using any of a number of formal rhyme schemes, typically having ten syllables per line.

label: improvisation
definition: The action of creating or performing something spontaneously without preparation, especially in music, theater, or dance.

label: genre
definition: A category of artistic composition characterized by similarities in form, style, or subject matter.

label: epic
definition: A long poem, typically one derived from ancient oral tradition, narrating the deeds and adventures of heroic or legendary figures.

label: chorus
definition: A part of a song that is repeated after each verse; a large organized group of singers, especially one that performs together.

label: opera
definition: A dramatic work in one or more acts set to music for singers and instrumentalists, combining text, music, drama, and often dance.

label: abstract
definition: Existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence; art that does not attempt to represent external reality.

label: baroque
definition: Relating to or denoting a style of European architecture, music, and art of the 17th and 18th centuries characterized by ornate detail.

label: lyric
definition: Expressing the writer's emotions in an imaginative and beautiful way; the words of a song.

label: allegory
definition: A story, poem, or picture that can be interpreted to reveal a hidden meaning, typically a moral or political one.

label: Impressionism
definition: A 19th-century art movement characterized by relatively small, thin, visible brushstrokes and emphasis on accurate depiction of light and its changing qualities.

label: harmony
definition: The combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce chords and chord progressions having a pleasing effect.

label: fable
definition: A short story, typically with animals as characters, conveying a moral lesson.

label: virtuoso
definition: A person highly skilled in music or another artistic pursuit; showing exceptional technical skill.

label: mosaic
definition: A picture or pattern produced by arranging together small colored pieces of hard material such as stone, tile, or glass.

label: concerto
definition: A musical composition for a solo instrument or instruments accompanied by an orchestra, especially one in three movements.

label: folklore
definition: The traditional beliefs, customs, and stories of a community, passed through the generations by word of mouth.

label: perspective
definition: The art of drawing solid objects on a two-dimensional surface so as to give the right impression of their height, width, depth, and position.

label: sonata
definition: A composition for an instrumental soloist, often with piano accompaniment, typically in several movements with one or more in sonata form.

label: anthology
definition: A published collection of poems or other pieces of writing or music by different authors or composers.

label: geography
definition: The study of Earth's physical features, climate, population, and the relationships between people and their environments.

label: desert
definition: A barren area of landscape with little precipitation, typically receiving less than 250mm of rain annually, characterized by extreme temperatures.

label: city
definition: A large human settlement with significant population density, complex infrastructure, and serving as a center for commerce, culture, and governance.

label: climate
definition: The long-term pattern of weather conditions in a particular area, including temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure, wind, and precipitation.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'klima' (region, zone), from 'klinein' (to slope, incline)

label: terrain
definition: A stretch of land, especially with regard to its physical features and characteristics.

label: plateau
definition: An area of relatively flat land at a high elevation, typically with steep sides.

label: valley
definition: A low area of land between hills or mountains, typically with a river or stream flowing through it.

label: volcano
definition: A rupture in the Earth's crust that allows hot lava, volcanic ash, and gases to escape from a magma chamber below the surface.

metadata.etymology: From Italian 'volcano', from Latin 'Vulcanus' (Roman god of fire)

label: peninsula
definition: A piece of land that is almost entirely surrounded by water but connected to the mainland on one side.

label: archipelago
definition: A group or chain of islands clustered together in a sea or ocean.

label: canyon
definition: A deep gorge with steep sides, typically carved by river erosion over geological time.

metadata.etymology: From Spanish 'cañón' (tube, gorge), from Latin 'canna' (reed, tube)

label: coastline
definition: The outline or boundary where land meets a body of water, especially the sea.

label: precipitation
definition: Any form of water - liquid or solid - falling from the sky, including rain, snow, sleet, and hail.

label: humidity
definition: The amount of water vapor present in the air, typically expressed as a percentage of saturation.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'humidus' (moist, wet), from 'humere' (to be moist)

label: latitude
definition: The angular distance of a place north or south of the earth's equator, measured in degrees.

label: longitude
definition: The angular distance of a place east or west of the prime meridian, measured in degrees.

label: equator
definition: An imaginary line drawn around the earth at 0 degrees latitude, equidistant from the North and South Poles, dividing the earth into hemispheres.

metadata.etymology: From Medieval Latin 'aequator', from Latin 'aequare' (to make equal)

label: tropics
definition: The region of Earth surrounding the equator between the Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn, characterized by warm climate year-round.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'tropikos' (of the turning), referring to sun's turning at solstices

label: temperate
definition: Relating to or denoting a climate region with mild temperatures, distinct seasons, and moderate rainfall.

label: tundra
definition: A vast, flat, treeless Arctic region with permanently frozen subsoil and low-growing vegetation.

metadata.etymology: From Russian 'tundra', from Kildin Sami 'tūndâr' (uplands, treeless mountain)

label: savanna
definition: A grassy plain in tropical and subtropical regions with few trees, experiencing seasonal rainfall and dry periods.

label: oasis
definition: A fertile spot in a desert where water is found, supporting vegetation and habitation.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'oasis', from Egyptian 'wḥꜣt' (fertile spot in desert)

label: glacier
definition: A large, slow-moving mass of ice formed from accumulated snow over many years.

label: erosion
definition: The gradual wearing away of land surface by natural forces such as water, wind, ice, or gravity.

label: urbanization
definition: The process by which towns and cities grow as more people move from rural to urban areas.

label: metropolis
definition: A very large, densely populated city, typically the chief city of a country or region.

label: suburb
definition: A residential area on the outskirts of a city or large town, typically quieter and less densely populated.

label: population
definition: All the inhabitants of a particular place, considered as a collective group.

label: migration
definition: The movement of people or animals from one region or country to another, often seasonally or permanently.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'migratio', from 'migrare' (to move from one place to another)

label: monsoon
definition: A seasonal prevailing wind in South Asia, especially bringing heavy rains in summer.

metadata.etymology: From Dutch 'monssoen', from Portuguese 'monção', from Arabic 'mawsim' (season)

label: altitude
definition: The height of an object or point in relation to sea level or ground level.

label: fjord
definition: A long, narrow, deep inlet of the sea between high cliffs, formed by glacial erosion.

metadata.etymology: From Norwegian 'fjord', from Old Norse 'fjǫrðr' (inlet, estuary)

label: delta
definition: A triangular or fan-shaped area of deposited sediment at the mouth of a river, formed by river sediments.

metadata.etymology: From Greek letter 'delta' (Δ), describing the triangular shape

label: watershed
definition: An area of land that drains all streams and rainfall to a common outlet such as a river, bay, or ocean.

label: ecosystem
definition: A biological community of interacting organisms and their physical environment functioning as a unit.

label: continental
definition: Relating to or characteristic of a continent, especially regarding climate with extreme temperature variations.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'continens' (continuous land), from 'continere' (to hold together)

label: maritime
definition: Connected with the sea, especially in relation to seafaring, naval, or climate moderated by ocean proximity.

label: arid
definition: Having little or no rain; extremely dry climate with insufficient moisture to support most plant life.

metadata.etymology: From Latin 'aridus' (dry, parched), from 'arere' (to be dry)

label: topography
definition: The arrangement of natural and artificial physical features of an area, or the detailed mapping and charting of these features.

label: deity
definition: A god or goddess in a polytheistic religion; a divine being worthy of worship.

label: pantheon
definition: All the gods of a particular religion or mythology collectively; a temple dedicated to all gods.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'pantheion' (temple of all gods), pan (all) + theos (god)

label: Zeus
definition: The king of the gods in ancient Greek mythology, ruler of Mount Olympus and god of the sky and thunder.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Zeus', possibly from Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'sky' or 'shine'

label: Odin
definition: The chief god in Norse mythology, associated with wisdom, war, death, and magic; ruler of Asgard.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Óðinn', related to 'óðr' (fury, poetry, mind)

label: Jade Emperor
definition: The supreme deity in Chinese folk religion and Taoism, ruler of heaven and all realms of existence.

metadata.etymology: Chinese 'Yù Huáng Dà Dì' (jade + emperor + great + sovereign)

label: Olympus
definition: The highest mountain in Greece, believed to be the home of the twelve major gods in Greek mythology.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Olympos', possibly pre-Greek origin meaning 'mountain'

label: Valhalla
definition: A majestic hall in Norse mythology where warriors who died in battle are taken by Valkyries to feast with Odin.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Valhǫll' (hall of the slain), valr (slain) + hǫll (hall)

label: Kunlun
definition: A sacred mountain in Chinese mythology, believed to be the dwelling place of immortals and the Queen Mother of the West.

label: Hercules
definition: A divine hero in Greek mythology, son of Zeus, known for his extraordinary strength and twelve labors.

metadata.etymology: Roman name for Greek 'Heracles', meaning 'glory of Hera'

label: Thor
definition: The Norse god of thunder, storms, and strength, wielder of the hammer Mjölnir and protector of mankind.

label: Nüwa
definition: A goddess in Chinese mythology credited with creating humanity and repairing the pillar of heaven.

label: immortal
definition: A being that lives forever or cannot die; in mythology, often refers to gods or those who achieved eternal life.

label: prophecy
definition: A prediction of future events, often delivered by a deity or oracle; a divine message about what will happen.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'prophēteia' (gift of interpreting the will of gods)

label: oracle
definition: A priest or priestess through whom a deity speaks; a shrine where such prophecies are given.

label: Titan
definition: One of the elder gods in Greek mythology who ruled before the Olympians; a being of enormous size and power.

label: Ragnarök
definition: The apocalyptic battle in Norse mythology resulting in the death of major gods and the rebirth of the world.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'ragnarǫk' (fate of the gods), ragna (gods) + rǫk (fate)

label: Odyssey
definition: An epic Greek poem attributed to Homer, describing the ten-year journey of Odysseus returning home after the Trojan War.

label: Valkyrie
definition: Female figures in Norse mythology who choose which warriors die in battle and escort them to Valhalla.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'valkyrja' (chooser of the slain), valr (slain) + kyrja (chooser)

label: phoenix
definition: A mythical bird that cyclically regenerates by burning itself and rising from its own ashes, symbolizing rebirth.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'phoinix' (purple-red, Phoenician), associated with Egyptian mythology

label: underworld
definition: The realm of the dead in various mythologies; the subterranean world where souls go after death.

label: Hades
definition: The Greek god of the underworld and the dead; also the name of the underworld realm itself.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Haidēs', possibly meaning 'the unseen one'

label: Loki
definition: A trickster god in Norse mythology, shape-shifter and companion of the gods who ultimately brings about Ragnarök.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Loki', possibly related to 'lúka' (to close, end)

label: Monkey King
definition: A legendary figure from Chinese mythology, the main character in Journey to the West, known for his strength and magical powers.

label: epic
definition: A long narrative poem celebrating heroic deeds and events significant to a culture or nation.

label: Odysseus
definition: Greek hero famous for his cunning and intelligence, protagonist of Homer's Odyssey and key figure in the Trojan War.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Odysseus', possibly meaning 'wrathful' or 'to be angry at'

label: Achilles
definition: The greatest Greek warrior in the Trojan War, nearly invulnerable except for his heel, protagonist of Homer's Iliad.

label: Trojan War
definition: A legendary conflict between Greeks and Trojans, sparked by Paris's abduction of Helen, lasting ten years.

metadata.etymology: Named after Troy (Greek 'Troia'), the city under siege

label: Athena
definition: Greek goddess of wisdom, warfare, handicraft, and strategic battle; patron deity of Athens.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Athēnā', possibly pre-Greek origin, related to Athens

label: Poseidon
definition: Greek god of the sea, earthquakes, and horses; one of the twelve Olympians and brother of Zeus.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Poseidōn', possibly 'husband of the earth' or 'lord of waters'

label: Apollo
definition: Greek god of music, poetry, prophecy, healing, and the sun; one of the most important Olympian deities.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Apollōn', possibly meaning 'destroyer' or from 'apellai' (assembly)

label: Artemis
definition: Greek goddess of the hunt, wilderness, wild animals, and the moon; twin sister of Apollo.

label: Freya
definition: Norse goddess of love, beauty, fertility, war, and death; leader of the Valkyries and practitioner of seidr magic.

label: Fenrir
definition: A monstrous wolf in Norse mythology, son of Loki, destined to kill Odin during Ragnarök.

label: Yggdrasil
definition: The immense sacred tree in Norse mythology that connects the nine worlds, central to Norse cosmology.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse, possibly 'Yggr's (Odin's) horse', referring to the gallows/tree

label: Midgard
definition: The realm of humans in Norse mythology, one of the nine worlds connected by Yggdrasil, located in the center.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Miðgarðr' (middle enclosure), mið (middle) + garðr (enclosure)

label: Asgard
definition: The realm of the Aesir gods in Norse mythology, home to Odin, Thor, and other deities, connected to Midgard by Bifrost.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Ásgarðr' (enclosure of the Aesir), áss (god) + garðr (enclosure)

label: Mjölnir
definition: Thor's magical hammer in Norse mythology, capable of leveling mountains and always returning to his hand.

metadata.etymology: From Old Norse 'Mjǫllnir', possibly meaning 'crusher' or 'lightning'

label: Medusa
definition: A Gorgon in Greek mythology with snakes for hair whose gaze turns people to stone; slain by Perseus.

label: Perseus
definition: Greek hero and demigod who slew Medusa, rescued Andromeda, and founded Mycenae; son of Zeus.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Perseus', possibly meaning 'destroyer' or 'to sack'

label: Minotaur
definition: A creature with the head of a bull and body of a man in Greek mythology, imprisoned in the Labyrinth of Crete.

label: Theseus
definition: Greek hero and king of Athens who slew the Minotaur, united Attica, and had many legendary adventures.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'Thēseus', possibly meaning 'to set' or 'establisher'

label: siren
definition: Dangerous creatures in Greek mythology who lured sailors with enchanting music and voices to shipwreck on rocky coasts.

metadata.etymology: From Greek 'seirēn', possibly from 'seira' (rope, cord) or related to 'sear' (dry, parched)

label: chimera
definition: A fire-breathing hybrid creature in Greek mythology with a lion's head, goat's body, and serpent's tail.

label: Hydra
definition: A multi-headed serpent in Greek mythology that regrows two heads for each one cut off; slain by Hercules.

label: centaur
definition: A creature in Greek mythology with the upper body of a human and the lower body and legs of a horse.

label: Cyclops
definition: A member of a race of giants in Greek mythology with a single eye in the middle of their forehead.

label: Fuxi
definition: A culture hero in Chinese mythology, credited with creating the eight trigrams, fishing, hunting, and domestication.

metadata.etymology: Chinese 'Fúxī', ancient legendary sovereign and cultural hero

label: Pangu
definition: The first living being and creator of the universe in Chinese mythology, who separated heaven and earth from chaos.
